title: Support Evidence, Authorization, and Troubleshooting Method · Guided Service Studio
summary: A guided support bench extends causal support incident record while retaining authorization, safety, baseline, privacy, accessibility, rollback, verification, documentation, and user communication. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of causal support incident record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate user goals, technician authority, system ownership, policy, safety, privacy, and escalation before touching state.
Apply identify, theorize, test, plan, implement, verify, prevent, and document as an evidence loop rather than a memorized script.
Record expected behavior, observed behavior, user impact, scope, frequency, severity, and a reproducible baseline without blaming the user.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Role Boundary
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A vague intermittent ticket triggers an unauthorized familiar fix before impact, baseline, recent change, prediction, rollback, or changed-case proof exists. The workshop variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Role Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate user goals, technician authority, system ownership, policy, safety, privacy, and escalation before touching state. For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Role Boundary, keep Role Boundary connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A vague intermittent ticket triggers an unauthorized familiar fix before impact, baseline, recent change, prediction, rollback, or changed-case proof exists. The workshop variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Role Boundary condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: CompTIA A+ Core 1 220-1201 Exam Objectives — Controls every current Core 1 mobile, networking, hardware, virtualization, cloud, and troubleshooting objective and weighting.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Role Boundary, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Role Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Role Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A vague intermittent ticket triggers an unauthorized familiar fix before impact, baseline, recent change, prediction, rollback, or changed-case proof exists. The workshop variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Role Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: A support ticket automatically authorizes every diagnostic and repair action.

options.text: A support ticket automatically authorizes every diagnostic and repair action. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Role Boundary.

For commit to the mission model, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof. This does not satisfy Role Boundary for causal support incident record.
State the Role Boundary preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.

  user-impact: [replace with the bounded user outcome]
  safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, and stop rule]
  hypothesis: [replace with a falsifiable cause and competing explanation]
  test: [replace with one reversible discriminating simulator test]
  observation: [replace with the actual result and rejected inference]

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Verify Document structural check is only one gate. causal support incident record must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

stimulus.title: Verify Document failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: The absence of the original symptom immediately after repair proves durable resolution.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify original and changed cases, check side effects, preserve evidence, state remaining uncertainty, and write an accessible handoff.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Verify Document condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Verify Document output and hide the violated causal support incident record assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Verify Document.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof. This does not satisfy Verify Document for causal support incident record.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify original and changed cases, check side effects, preserve evidence, state remaining uncertainty, and write an accessible handoff.
